chunks.text: [Login](https://www.advamed.org/auth/login/) [Resource Library](https://www.advamed.org/member-center/resource-library/) # State of the Nation 2025: Global Medical Device Recall Index Report * [Compliance](https://www.advamed.org/topics/compliance/) * [Global & Trade](https://www.advamed.org/topics/global-trade/) * [Regulatory Affairs](https://www.advamed.org/topics/regulatory-affairs/) [Access This Report](https://www.advamed.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/Sedgwick-Brand-Protection-Recall-Index-Report-Global-Medical-Device-Edition.pdf) **State of the Nation 2025: Global Medical Device Recall Index Report** **By Sedgwick** Gain unparalleled insight into one of the most highly regulated and rapidly evolving sectors—medical devices. This special edition of Sedgwick’s globally recognized *Recall Index Report* delivers data, trends, and strategic forecasts from 2024, with analysis spanning the U.S., EU/UK, and Australia. Whether you manufacture, distribute, or oversee compliance in the medical device space, this report provides critical recall statistics, regulatory updates, and legal developments that could significantly impact your risk exposure, product planning, and go-to-market strategy. With record-high recall volumes, new AI and telehealth regulatory guidelines, and sweeping global changes on the horizon, this is essential intelligence for anyone in the industry. Key insights include: * U.S. recalls reached a four-year high, with Class I recalls at a 15-year peak. * Software and device failure were leading causes of recalls across all regions. * Major legal and regulatory shifts, including AI guidance, decentralized clinical trials, and environmental mandates. * In-depth analysis of litigation, compliance challenges, and opportunities for innovation. Don’t wait for a product crisis to find out if you’re prepared. Use this report to benchmark your organization’s readiness, strengthen your brand’s defenses, and get ahead of what’s next. 👉 *Available exclusively toAdvaMed® members.* --- **Disclaimer:** This report is produced by Sedgwick and is provided as a benefit ofAdvaMed® Associate Membership.AdvaMed® does not endorse the content or conclusions of this report. Associate members are allowed to post whitepapers and reports to the resource center as part of their membership benefits. resource center as part of their membership benefits. chunks.text: global frameworks, and clearer distinctions between real-world data and evidence.](https://www.advamed.org/member-center/resource-library/clinical-evidence-for-ivd-medical-devices/) [News / Regulatory Affairs ### AdvaMed® Welcomes Senate Introduction of Bipartisan Medical Device Electronic Labeling Bill May 14, 2026 Washington, D.C.—AdvaMed, the Medtech Association, the world’s biggest trade association representing medtech innovators, welcomed Senate introduction of the bipartisan Medical Device Electronic Labeling Act (S. 4519), which would allow medtech instructions for use to be provided electronically, with paper copies being available upon request.](https://www.advamed.org/industry-updates/news/advamed-welcomes-senate-introduction-of-bipartisan-medical-device-electronic-labeling-bill/) [News / Regulatory Affairs / Small Business ### Bright Uro Receives FDA Clearance for Glean® Abdominal Sensor May 8, 2026 IRVINE, Calif., May 7, 2026 /PRNewswire/ — Bright Uro, a medical device company with a mission to transform care for lower urinary tract dysfunction (LUTD) through innovations in urodynamics, today announced that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has granted 510(k) clearance for the Glean® Abdominal Sensor. This new sensor expands the capabilities of the Glean® Urodynamics System to include multi-channel urodynamic studies. This comprehensive system enables wireless, catheter-free ambulatory urodynamics designed to support clinical decision making for patients with LUTD and other urological conditions.](https://www.advamed.org/industry-updates/news/bright-uro-receives-fda-clearance-for-glean-abdominal-sensor/) [News / Regulatory Affairs ### i-GENTIC AI Expands GENIE to Enforce Full FDA Compliance Lifecycle for Life Sciences May 4, 2026 PALO ALTO, CA, UNITED STATES, May 4, 2026 / EINPresswire.com / — Biopharma and medtech companies face a years-long process to get their products approved. Drug and device makers navigate a full spectrum of FDA and global regulatory frameworks to move innovation forward.](https://www.advamed.org/industry-updates/news/i-gentic-ai-expands-genie-to-enforce-full-fda-compliance-lifecycle-for-life-sciences/) [Blog / Emerging Policy Response Resources / Global & Trade / Tariffs ### IEEPA Tariff Refunds Summary – What MedTech Companies Need to Know April 29, 2026 After the Supreme Court struck down IEEPA tariffs, CBP launched the CAPE system to process refunds. Here’s what importers need to know about eligibility, submission, and timing.](https://www.advamed.org/2026/04/29/ieepa-tariff-refunds-webinar-summary/) [Blog / Coverage & Payment / Government & Legislative Affairs / Health Access / Regulatory Affairs ### **A Policy Win for Maternal Health Innovation Through FDA–CMS Collaboration** April 27, 2026 The United States faces a maternal health crisis demanding urgent action. The U.S. has the worst maternal outcomes among high income nations. Our maternal mortality rates far exceed peer countries’, despite the highest per capita health care spending.
